title: ficus gibbosa f. tuberculata
intro: Ficus gibbosa f. tuberculata, also known as the variegated bengkong fig, is a type of plant in the mulberry family (Moraceae) that is native to the tropical regions of Southeast Asia. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory effects. The health benefits of Ficus gibbosa f. tuberculata include its ability to aid in wound healing, reduce fever, and alleviate digestive issues. Ficus gibbosa f. tuberculata is often used in herbal preparations, such as teas and infusions, to take advantage of its medicinal properties, although further research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
